summary: vite-common-utils ships obfuscated loader that fetches and executes remote JS from a personal GitHub repo
details: Package advertises itself as a Vite utility library but its only export, `loadFilbetScriptSilently`, creates a `<script>` element with `src` set to https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/gongben2024/network-security@main/src/filbet.js and appends it to `document.documentElement`, causing the browser to execute whatever the `@main` branch of that personal GitHub account currently serves. The loader code in dist/index.js is heavily obfuscated (string-array indirection, hex-named identifiers like `_0x1f55d0`), the destination is an unpinned mutable branch on a personal account (gongben2024/network-security), and neither the package name (`vite-common-utils`) nor its description matches a 'filbet' loader. Any consumer application that calls the export — or any framework that auto-invokes it — will run attacker-controlled JavaScript in the end user's browser, with the attacker able to swap the payload at any time by pushing to the repo's main branch.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
